title: Migrate: emit FK constraints via ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT post-pass
description: Follow-up to forge-u0w. Currently renderCreateTable emits FOREIGN KEY constraints INLINE in CREATE TABLE, which makes the migrate [ManagedTable] list order load-bearing: a table whose References FK targets another must be created AFTER its target, else Postgres fails at migrate time with 'relation does not exist'. Fix: move FK emission to a separate ALTER TABLE <child> ADD CONSTRAINT ... FOREIGN KEY (col) REFERENCES tgt(pk) post-pass run AFTER all CREATE TABLEs in the additive plan (Manifest.Migrate.migrate/migrateUp). This removes the ordering requirement and also handles circular references. Flagged by the forge-u0w final review (Important). Until then, the ordering requirement is documented in migrateUp's haddock.

title: Per-field read/write policy modifiers (FieldAutogenerated, FieldWriteOnly) -> distinct Read/Write representations
description:
Extend the HKD field vocabulary with per-field policy modifiers so one declaration projects into DIFFERENT representations per direction (Read vs Write/Insert/Update). Today every field is 'Field f a' (same in every context). Proposed:

data UserT f = User
  { userId       :: FieldAutogenerated f (Pk Int)
  , userName     :: Field f Text
  , userEmail    :: Field f (Nullable Text)
  , userPassword :: FieldWriteOnly f Text
  , userCreated  :: FieldAutogenerated f UTCTime
  , userUpdated  :: FieldAutogenerated f UTCTime
  }

Semantics by direction:
- Field: present on Read and Write.
- FieldAutogenerated: present on Read; ABSENT/optional on Write (DB fills via serial / DEFAULT now()); value captured back via RETURNING. (Serial PK + created_at fill on INSERT; updated_at on UPDATE.)
- FieldWriteOnly (e.g. password hash): ABSENT on Read (never SELECTed into the value); present on Write.

Net: typed Insert/Update/Read shapes fall out of one record. A user value you read never carries the password; an insert value never carries the autogenerated id/timestamps.
design: DESIGN COMPLETE — see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-forge-2am-field-policy-design.md. Settled: two axes (read/readwrite direction + composable payload markers Generated/Default/Secret/Nullable; WriteOnly DROPPED, secrecy is serialization-only via Secret). Fork (a): one UserT f + Field type family projects Read/Create/Update with Omitted fillers (Omitted in Create/Update only; Read=full row). Scheme M (one Field family). Patch a = Keep | Set a. Two update paths (Patch for API endpoints, mutate-and-flush diff for pipelines) converge on one runUpdate executor. Profunctors stay leaf-only; no product-profunctors. Generic neutral-skeleton constructors (blankCreate/noChange) for ergonomics. Relationships parked to forge-u0w.
acceptance_criteria: A design doc that settles: the modifier set (Field / FieldAutogenerated / FieldWriteOnly, and whether FieldReadOnly/FieldDefault are needed); the context model (Read/Write, or finer Insert/Update/Patch); how absent is represented (fork a vs b); how Read/Write types and their codecs are generated; and how the Unit-of-Work handles autogenerated refresh + write-only writes.
notes: IMPLEMENTED and merged to main (commits cd12e96..05f6256). 11 TDD tasks + integration rework; final review MERGEABLE (0 critical/important). manifest:spec 166/166, whole-workspace build green. As-built deltas in the spec's addendum. Deferrals tracked in a follow-up bead; relationships in forge-u0w. Design AC met; closing-eligible once deferrals are tracked.
close_reason: Design AC met (spec + as-built addendum) and fully implemented + merged to main (cd12e96..05f6256, 166/166 tests, final review clean). Deferrals tracked in forge-0m6; relationships in forge-u0w.

title: evals-ui/zinc.lock can silently desync from the root zinc.lock on `zinc update`
description: manifest-evals/evals-ui is a dual-purpose member: it is a member of the forge root workspace AND its own standalone workspace for the wasm32-wasi build, carrying its own manifest-evals/evals-ui/zinc.lock. Shared deps (e.g. aeson 2.3.0.0) are duplicated between forge/zinc.lock and the evals-ui lock and currently match exactly. A future `zinc update` that bumps a shared dependency in the root lock will NOT touch the evals-ui lock, silently diverging the wasm build's dependency set until someone re-syncs it by hand. Pre-existing design (predates the monorepo migration); surfaced during the forge consolidation.
design: Options: (a) add a CI/check step that asserts shared dep versions match between forge/zinc.lock and manifest-evals/evals-ui/zinc.lock; (b) restructure so evals-ui derives its wasm lock from the root lock instead of hand-maintaining a copy; (c) at minimum, document the sync requirement next to the evals-ui dual-purpose comment.
acceptance_criteria: Drift between the two locks is either prevented or detected automatically, or the manual sync requirement is clearly documented.
close_reason: AC met: drift now DETECTED automatically by LockSyncSpec (manifest-evals suite, runs on every zinc test) — asserts shared-dep sha256 match, strict. On first run it caught a REAL pre-existing drift (integer-conversion 0.1.1: root regenerated sha vs stale evals-ui subtree sha), which is fixed in commit cf63845. Test green (40/40 shared entries match). Eliminating the duplication (deriving the wasm lock) needs a zinc target-aware-lock-filtering feature — out of scope, noted in the spec for upstream.

title: Root flake.nix sets no SSL_CERT_FILE; live HTTPS eval runs inside nix develop may fail TLS verification
description: forge/flake.nix's devShell provides GHC + system libs but does not set SSL_CERT_FILE / include pkgs.cacert. crucible makes outbound HTTPS calls (e.g. the Anthropic API); manifest-evals eval runs that hit live endpoints from inside `nix develop` may fail certificate verification. None of the three original flakes set it, so this is NOT a regression from the migration — flagged for eval-run reliability.
design: Add pkgs.cacert to the devShell packages and export SSL_CERT_FILE=${pkgs.cacert}/etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt in the shellHook. Only needed if eval runs perform live HTTPS from within the dev shell.
acceptance_criteria: A crucible HTTPS request succeeds from within `nix develop` without manual cert configuration.
notes: Applied: pkgs.cacert added to devShell packages; SSL_CERT_FILE exported to ${pkgs.cacert}/etc/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt in shellHook. VERIFIED: flake evaluates (nix flake check); inside 'nix develop', SSL_CERT_FILE is set and the bundle file exists (nss-cacert-3.117). NOT verified end-to-end: a live crucible HTTPS call (needs API creds) — note curl is NOT a valid proxy here (nixpkgs curl carries its own CA bundle, verifies with or without SSL_CERT_FILE); the fix specifically enables the Haskell http-client-tls/x509-system path which reads SSL_CERT_FILE.
close_reason: Fix applied + verified at the mechanism level: pkgs.cacert in devShell, SSL_CERT_FILE exported to a valid CA bundle, flake evaluates. The exact AC (a live crucible HTTPS call) was not exercised end-to-end (needs API creds/network); the change is the standard x509-system fix and is correct. Reopen if a live eval run is wanted.

title: Migrate: 63-byte-identifier-safe reconciliation for FK + index names
description: Latent bug flagged by the forge-irh final review, shared by FK and index reconciliation. foreignKeysForTable generates the constraint name <table>_<col>_fkey and indexesForTable generates <table>_<cols>_<suffix>_idx, then checks 'name notElem liveNames'. If the generated name exceeds Postgres's 63-byte identifier limit, PG stores a TRUNCATED name; the untruncated generated name is never found in the live set, so the reconciliation re-issues ADD CONSTRAINT / CREATE INDEX on every migrate -> the second run fails with 'already exists'. Fix: truncate the generated name to 63 bytes (matching PG's storage) before the notElem check, in BOTH foreignKeysForTable (Manifest.Migrate) and indexesForTable. Unlikely with current short snake_case names; low priority.

title: renderInsert: emit DEFAULT VALUES for all-DB-assigned tables
description: Pre-existing limitation exposed by forge-u0w. An entity whose only columns are DB-assigned (e.g. a single serial PK) makes renderInsert produce 'INSERT INTO t () VALUES ()', which Postgres rejects as a syntax error — such an entity cannot be add-ed. Fix: when the insert column list is empty, emit 'INSERT INTO t DEFAULT VALUES RETURNING ...' instead. Surfaced when the forge-u0w cascade test needed a non-serial dummy column on its Owner fixture to be insertable. See manifest/manifest-core/src/Manifest/Core/Sql.hs renderInsert and manifest/src/Manifest/Session.hs insertCreate.

title: ReferencesOnDelete: column-level DB-enforced ON DELETE policy
description: Follow-up to forge-u0w (References FK markers). Add a ReferencesOnDelete (p :: OnDelete) t marker variant that emits ON DELETE CASCADE|SET NULL|RESTRICT in the FK constraint, letting the DB enforce the delete policy so the parent-side app-level cascadeRules can be dropped. Needs: promote OnDelete to a kind (DataKinds), a KnownOnDelete reflection class, and the policy threaded into fkDDL. Design already sketched in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-29-forge-u0w-references-fk-markers-design.md (Out of scope section). An optimisation over the app-level cascades, not a capability gap.

title: forge-2am follow-ups: generated-on-update stamping, RETURNING refresh, Secret/Masked codec wiring
description: Deferred items from the forge-2am field-policy implementation (merged to main). See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-forge-2am-field-policy-design.md §7 + as-built notes. (1) Generated-on-update stamping: touchGenerated currently a no-op; updated_at-style columns should be flush-stamped on UPDATE. (2) RETURNING refresh: after insertCreate/patch, refresh DB-generated values (serial id, defaults) back into the in-memory entity. (3) Secret/Masked codec wiring: the Masked primitive + Secret marker exist, but Secret-marked fields are not yet auto-masked in entity JSON derivation. (4) Generated insert-vs-update distinction (created_at once vs updated_at every write).
acceptance_criteria: Each deferred item either implemented with tests or explicitly descoped with rationale.
notes: Item 1+4 (generated-on-update): DONE via Touched marker (renderUpdate auto-stamps now()). Item 2 insert-side RETURNING refresh: already implemented in insertCreate (setBaseline on RETURNING row) — no work. Item 2 update-side: stamp-only via Touched, no RETURNING. Item 3 Secret/Masked entity-JSON wiring: DESCOPED — manifest has no entity-JSON derivation layer to hook into (only Json/Aeson column wrappers + standalone Masked). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-28-forge-0m6-touched-lifecycle-design.md
close_reason: Touched marker implemented (4 tasks, reviewed clean, final review ready-to-merge); insert-side RETURNING already done; Secret/Masked entity-JSON wiring descoped (no derivation layer). 6 TouchedSpec tests green.

title: References/FK relationship markers for the HKD field vocabulary
description: Surfaced while designing forge-2am (per-field policy markers). The two-axis model (read/readwrite direction + composable payload markers) accommodates a 'References T' marker so foreign-key relationships are declared in the same grammar as Pk/Generated/Secret. Parked out of forge-2am to keep that spec focused. Ties to the existing Manifest.Core.Cascade rules: a References marker should drive both the FK column/DDL and the cascade policy. Design questions: cardinality (One/Opt/Many), how the marker names the target entity, how it interacts with the Read/Create/Update projections (an FK is a readwrite scalar column), and the relationship-loading strategy (eager/lazy). See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-forge-2am-field-policy-design.md §1, §7.
design: Marker 'References T' on the payload (Field f (References Author)). Cardinality via marker variants or a separate type. Must reconcile with HasRelation (manifest/spec) and Manifest.Core.Cascade. FK is a readwrite scalar at the column level.
acceptance_criteria: A design settling the References marker shape, cardinality encoding, target naming, projection behaviour (FK as readwrite column), and how it drives DDL + cascade rules.
close_reason: References FK marker implemented (5 tasks, reviewed clean, final review ready-to-merge with documented ordering limitation). Marker types FK column as target PrimKey; migration emits FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES DDL; DB-enforced + composes with app cascades. ON DELETE, ADD-CONSTRAINT post-pass, and empty-INSERT fix filed as follow-ups. 179 tests green.

title: Evaluate barbies for the HKD Core (bmap/btraverse/bzipWith, Bare/Covered)
description:
Manifest hand-rolls its HKD field operations with GHC.Generics. The barbies library packages the same 'record parameterised by a functor' shape and derives lawful, named combinators that map directly onto mechanisms we built by hand:

- bmap (FunctorB): change the wrapping functor / 'pick a face' (Identity -> metadata, Identity -> encode).
- btraverse (TraversableB): applicative row decode (our codec) as a one-liner.
- bzipWith / bprod (ApplicativeB): snapshot-diff baseline vs handed-back value (the column-by-column flush fold).
- ConstraintsB / AllB DbType: per-field DbType dictionaries the codec needs.
- Bare / Covered / Wear (Barbies.Bare): a plain record with NO Identity wrapper at all - cleaner than 'type User = UserT Identity'; bstrip/bcover move between bare and covered.

Surfaced while drafting the manifest category-theory blog series (the 'showing the working' post): four load-bearing mechanisms in the series are barbies one-liners.

LIMITS (why it's at most a partial replacement):
- barbies changes the WRAPPER f; it does not interpret the CONTENTS. Our Base family stripping PrimaryKey (Serial Int) -> Int, the PK-is-first-field convention, and serial/nullable semantics are still ours.
- The query-expression face is not free unless the expression type already exists.
- Ethos tension: spec says 'thin owned Core; borrow ideas, not deps'. barbies is small and well-regarded but adds a dependency and surfaces its classes in signatures.
design: Tension to resolve: owned-thin-Core/no-deps ethos vs. deleting hand-rolled Generics folds. Likely options: (a) adopt barbies fully; (b) adopt only Barbies.Bare for the plain record type + btraverse/bzipWith for codec+diff; (c) keep hand-rolled. Bare/Covered is the cleanest single win (removes Identity noise from every signature).
acceptance_criteria: A written recommendation (adopt fully / adopt Bare+codec only / keep hand-rolled) with rough diff/impact on Manifest.Core.Table, the codec, and the snapshot-diff fold, and a note on dependency cost.
notes: Recommendation: KEEP HAND-ROLLED. Borrow the barbies vocabulary (bmap/btraverse/bzipWith as conceptual anchors), not the dependency. Full write-up: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-forge-i14-barbies-evaluation.md. Key findings: (1) the 'Bare/Covered removes Identity noise' premise is void — Field Identity a = Base a is already bare + marker-stripped; (2) barbies' fixed-field-set/uniform-f model can't express per-context field-type changes, and forge-2am's read/readwrite+policy markers make the mismatch total; (3) only ~70 LOC of clean Generics is replaceable, against the thin-owned-Core/no-deps ethos. Also: do NOT add product-profunctors (three projected shapes can't fit a 2-param profunctor).
close_reason: Written recommendation delivered: keep hand-rolled, borrow vocabulary not deps. Spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-forge-i14-barbies-evaluation.md

value: In the forge repo, 'bd dolt push' is NOT configured (it just prints a usage banner). Beads state syncs via the git-tracked .beads/issues.jsonl file — commit it and 'git push'. The session-close 'bd dolt push' step is effectively a no-op here; git push is what persists bead state (consistent with the 'chore(bd): close ...' commits in history).
